extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the sprint after Quick-Step controlled a 3000m altitude stage alone, beating NIZZOLO Giacomo in the final straight.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step controlled entire stage in single file without peloton cooperation, burning SERRY and ASGREEN on climbs preemptively.
PÖSTLBERGER attempted solo acceleration at 72km/h before 2km to go, demonstrating aggressive leadout disruption tactics.
Sky worked only until summit of climbs then withdrew, revealing unwillingness to aid sprint teams in altitude stages.
CONSONNI and VIVIANI exchanged looks suggesting Italian coalition despite different teams, indicating informal nationalism before sprint.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: First Grand Tour stage win on 3000m altitude course demonstrates evolved climbing capacity for pure sprinter
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: Lost head-to-head sprint against compatriot despite Quick-Step committing all resources to control
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Positioned perfectly with WALSCHEID leadout but did not contest final 200m, suggesting selective sprint targeting
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: Young rider pulled all day on altitude stage for Quick-Step, showing unexpected domestique endurance capacity
rider_signals.PÖSTLBERGER Lukas: positive: Solo attack late in stage at extreme speed shows residual form from national championship campaign
